title: Define MAP sync message types
content:
Define the shared `memory:sync` and `skill:sync` message type interfaces that both OpenHive and swarms use.

```typescript
type MapSyncMessage = {
  type: "memory:sync" | "skill:sync";
  resource_id: string;       // syncable_resources ID
  agent_id: string;          // who pushed
  commit_hash: string;       // git commit
  timestamp: string;         // ISO 8601
}
```

This is the contract between swarms and OpenHive. Decide where these types live (shared package, or duplicated in each project).

Implements [[s-78fe]].

feedback.content: Added `MapSyncMessage` interface and `MapSyncMessageType` to `src/map/types.ts`. Added `memory:sync` and `skill:sync` to `WSEventType` in `src/types.ts`. Types live in the OpenHive package alongside other MAP types — swarm implementations will import or duplicate the interface.

title: OpenHive: Subscribe to swarm MAP endpoints for sync messages
content:
When a swarm registers via `POST /map/swarms`, OpenHive should subscribe to the swarm's `map_endpoint` using the declared `map_transport` (websocket / http-sse / ndjson).

OpenHive filters for `memory:sync` and `skill:sync` message types. On receiving either:

1. **Bookkeeping** — update `syncable_resources` sync state:
   - `updateResourceSyncState(resource_id, commit_hash, agent_id)`
   - `createSyncEvent({ resource_id, commit_hash, pusher: agent_id, ... })`
2. **WebSocket broadcast** — emit on `resource:{type}:{id}` channel for web UI / non-MAP clients

This replaces webhooks as the primary sync signal for MAP-aware swarms. Webhooks remain as a parallel path for non-MAP git pushes.

Depends on [[i-5nl8]] for message type definitions.
Implements [[s-78fe]].

feedback.content:
Created `src/map/sync-listener.ts` with:
- `initMapSyncListener()` — connects to all online swarms at startup, subscribes to `mapHubEvents` for new registrations
- `handleSyncMessage()` — validates resource, updates sync state, creates audit event, broadcasts to local WebSocket channels
- WebSocket connection management with exponential backoff reconnection
- Wired into `server.ts` startup/shutdown lifecycle (guarded by `config.mapHub.enabled`)
- Exported from `src/map/index.ts`

title: OpenHive: Relay sync messages to subscribed swarms
content:
After OpenHive receives a `memory:sync` or `skill:sync` message from a swarm, it needs to relay the message to all swarms that are subscribed to that resource.

Flow:
1. Extract `resource_id` from the received message
2. Look up `resource_subscriptions` for that resource
3. Identify which swarms those subscribed agents belong to (agent → swarm mapping via `map_swarms.owner_agent_id` or `map_nodes`)
4. Send the message to each relevant swarm's `map_endpoint` using the swarm's declared `map_transport`
5. Skip sending back to the originating swarm

Depends on [[i-7ahu]] (inbound subscription must work first).
Implements [[s-78fe]].

feedback.content:
Added relay logic to `src/map/sync-listener.ts`:
- `relaySyncMessage()` — looks up resource subscribers, maps subscriber agents to their online swarms via `findSwarmsByOwnerAgentIds()`, sends the message to each swarm's open WebSocket connection (skipping the source swarm)
- Added `findSwarmsByOwnerAgentIds()` to `src/db/dal/map.ts` — queries online swarms owned by a set of agent IDs
- Relay is called from `handleSyncMessage()` after bookkeeping completes

title: Swarm: Emit memory:sync and skill:sync after tool operations

feedback.content:
Created `src/map/sync-client.ts` — a client library for swarm runtimes to participate in MAP sync. Provides:
- `emitMemorySync()` / `emitSkillSync()` — broadcast sync messages to connected clients (including OpenHive's sync listener)
- `handleIncomingConnection()` — register WebSocket clients connecting to the swarm's MAP endpoint
- Exported as `MapSyncClient` from `src/map/index.ts`

Since the swarm runtime (OpenSwarm) is a separate project, this module lives in OpenHive as an importable SDK. Swarm runtimes wire it into their tool lifecycle:
```typescript
const client = new MapSyncClient({ agent_id, hub_ws_url, owned_resources });
client.emitMemorySync({ resource_id: 'res_abc', commit_hash: 'abc123' });
```

title: Swarm: Handle received sync messages and trigger tool pulls
Swarm: Polling fallback for missed sync messages
Add bridge database schema (bridge_configs, channel_mappings, proxy_agents, message_mappings)
content:
Add the four new tables to the database schema as a new migration:

**Tables:**

1. `bridge_configs` — bridge instance configuration (name, platform, transport_mode, credentials_encrypted, status, error_message, owner_agent_id)
2. `bridge_channel_mappings` — platform channel → hive mapping (bridge_id, platform_channel_id, platform_channel_name, hive_name, direction, thread_mode)
3. `bridge_proxy_agents` — external user → OpenHive agent mapping (bridge_id, platform_user_id, agent_id, platform_display_name, platform_avatar_url)
4. `bridge_message_mappings` — platform message → post/comment mapping for thread tracking (bridge_id, platform_message_id, platform_channel_id, post_id, comment_id)

See spec [[s-9mbr]] for full DDL. Follow existing schema migration pattern in `src/db/schema.ts`. Add appropriate indexes on foreign keys and unique constraints.

feedback.content: Created `src/bridge/schema.ts` with 4 tables: `bridge_configs`, `bridge_channel_mappings`, `bridge_proxy_agents`, `bridge_message_mappings`. Registered as schema migration v17 in `src/db/index.ts`. All existing DB tests pass.

title: Define bridge adapter interface and shared types
content:
Create `src/bridge/types.ts` with the TypeScript interfaces and types for the bridge system:

- `BridgeAdapter` interface — `connect()`, `messages()`, `send()`, `disconnect()`
- `AdapterConfig` — mode, credentials, channelMappings, webhookBaseUrl
- `InboundMessage` — normalized inbound message with platformMessageId, author, content, thread, mentions, platformMeta
- `OutboundMessage` — text, attachments, replyTo
- `PlatformDestination` — platformChannelId, threadId
- `Attachment` — type, url, name, mimeType
- `ChannelMapping` — matches the DB table shape
- `BridgeConfig` — matches the DB table shape
- `BridgeStatus` — connecting, connected, error, disconnected
- `RunningBridge` — runtime state for an active bridge

Export from `src/bridge/index.ts`. These types are consumed by the bridge manager, adapters, and API routes.

feedback.content: Created `src/bridge/types.ts` with all interfaces: `BridgeAdapter`, `InboundMessage`, `OutboundMessage`, `PlatformDestination`, `BridgeConfig`, `ChannelMapping`, `ProxyAgent`, `MessageMapping`, `BridgePostMetadata`, and API input types. Exported via `src/bridge/index.ts`.

title: Add bridge instance configuration to OpenHive config
content:
Add bridge configuration to the OpenHive instance config schema:

```typescript
bridge: {
  enabled: boolean;             // master toggle, default false
  maxBridges: number;           // limit on concurrent bridges, default 10
  credentialEncryptionKey?: string; // override; defaults to derived from instance secret
  webhookBaseUrl?: string;      // public URL for webhook mode
}
```

Update the config type definitions, defaults, and validation. The bridge feature should be disabled by default.

title: Implement bridge DAL (data access layer) for bridge tables
content:
Create `src/db/dal/bridge.ts` with data access functions for the bridge tables. Follow the existing DAL pattern used by other modules (e.g., `src/db/dal/map.ts`).

**Functions needed:**

Bridge configs:
- `createBridge(config)` — insert new bridge config
- `getBridge(id)` — get bridge by ID
- `listBridges(ownerId?)` — list bridges, optionally filtered by owner
- `updateBridge(id, updates)` — update bridge config (status, credentials, etc.)
- `deleteBridge(id)` — delete bridge and cascade to mappings/proxy agents

Channel mappings:
- `addChannelMapping(bridgeId, mapping)` — add a channel mapping
- `getChannelMappings(bridgeId)` — list mappings for a bridge
- `getChannelMappingByPlatformChannel(bridgeId, platformChannelId)` — lookup for inbound routing
- `getChannelMappingsByHive(bridgeId, hiveName)` — lookup for outbound routing
- `deleteChannelMapping(id)` — remove a mapping

Proxy agents:
- `getOrCreateProxyAgent(bridgeId, platformUser)` — find existing or create new proxy agent
- `getProxyAgentByPlatformUser(bridgeId, platformUserId)` — lookup
- `listProxyAgents(bridgeId)` — list all proxy agents for a bridge

Message mappings:
- `recordMessageMapping(mapping)` — insert message mapping
- `getMessageMapping(bridgeId, platformMessageId)` — lookup for thread resolution
- `getMessageMappingByPost(bridgeId, postId)` — reverse lookup for outbound thread tracking

Depends on: bridge schema issue, bridge types issue.

title: Implement credential encryption for bridge configs
content:
Implement encryption/decryption for bridge platform credentials stored in `bridge_configs.credentials_encrypted`.

- Use AES-256-GCM for symmetric encryption
- Key derived from `config.bridge.credentialEncryptionKey` or fallback to instance secret
- `encryptCredentials(credentials: Record<string, string>, key: string): string` — returns base64-encoded ciphertext with IV and auth tag
- `decryptCredentials(encrypted: string, key: string): Record<string, string>` — returns plaintext credentials
- Credentials should never appear in logs or API responses (redact in bridge GET endpoints)

Create as `src/bridge/credentials.ts`.

title: Implement mention extraction for bridge inbound messages
content:
Implement the mention extraction logic that scans inbound message text for `@name` patterns and populates the `mentions` array on `InboundMessage`.

**Behavior:**
- Scan message text for `@name` patterns (word boundary delimited)
- Each adapter may also provide platform-native mentions (e.g., Slack `<@U0XYZ789>`) which the adapter resolves to display names before passing to the bridge core
- The bridge core extracts `@name` mentions from the text and deduplicates with any adapter-provided mentions
- Mentions are stored as-is (no validation against known agent/swarm names)
- Mentions are included in post metadata: `{ mentions: ["infra-swarm", "agent-claude"] }`

Create as a utility function in `src/bridge/mentions.ts`:
- `extractMentions(text: string, adapterMentions?: string[]): string[]`

Per spec [[s-9mbr]] Message Routing section — the bridge annotates but does not route. No swarm registry lookup.

title: Implement inbound message pipeline (platform → OpenHive)
content:
Implement the core inbound pipeline that processes normalized `InboundMessage` objects from adapters and creates posts/comments in OpenHive.

**Pipeline steps** (per spec [[s-9mbr]] Inbound Flow):

1. Resolve channel mapping — lookup `(bridge_id, platformChannelId)` in `bridge_channel_mappings`, skip if no mapping or direction is `outbound`
2. Resolve or create proxy agent — lookup/create via bridge DAL `getOrCreateProxyAgent()`
3. Extract mentions — call `extractMentions()` on message text
4. Determine post vs. comment based on `thread_mode`:
   - `post_per_message`: top-level = post, thread reply = comment (lookup parent via `bridge_message_mappings`)
   - `single_thread`: always comment on pinned post
   - `explicit_only`: check trigger pattern, skip non-matching
5. Create post or comment via OpenHive API/DAL (using proxy agent as author)
6. Set bridge metadata on post: `{ bridge, platform_message_id, platform_channel_id, mentions }`
7. Record message mapping in `bridge_message_mappings`
8. OpenHive's existing broadcast handles the WebSocket notification

Create as `src/bridge/inbound.ts` with a `processInboundMessage(bridgeId, message)` function.

Depends on: bridge DAL, mention extraction, bridge types.

feedback.content: Implemented inbound pipeline in `src/bridge/inbound.ts` with `processInboundMessage(bridgeId, message)`. Handles all three thread modes (post_per_message, single_thread, explicit_only). Creates proxy agents automatically on first message from a platform user. Extracts mentions and broadcasts to WebSocket. 16 tests covering skip conditions, post/comment creation, proxy agent reuse, thread replies, mention extraction, message mapping, and title truncation.

title: Implement outbound message pipeline (OpenHive → platform)
content:
Implement the outbound pipeline that subscribes to OpenHive WebSocket channels for mapped hives and relays new posts/comments back to the external platform.

**Pipeline steps** (per spec [[s-9mbr]] Outbound Flow):

1. Subscribe to WebSocket channels for all mapped hives (`hive:{name}`)
2. On `new_post` or `new_comment` event, check:
   a. Is the author a proxy agent owned by this bridge? Skip (avoid echo)
   b. Is the channel mapping direction `inbound`? Skip (read-only)
   c. Does the post/comment have bridge metadata from this bridge? Skip (avoid echo)
3. Resolve platform destination — hive → platform channel from mappings
4. For comments, look up parent post in `bridge_message_mappings` for thread ID
5. Format outbound message — author name, markdown-to-platform conversion, thread context
6. Call `adapter.send(destination, message)`
7. Record outbound message in `bridge_message_mappings` for future thread tracking

Create as `src/bridge/outbound.ts` with an `OutboundRelay` class that manages the WebSocket subscription and relays events.

Depends on: bridge DAL, bridge types.

feedback.content: Implemented outbound pipeline in `src/bridge/outbound.ts` with `processOutboundEvent(bridgeConfig, mappings, event)`. Filters proxy agent events (echo prevention), respects channel direction, resolves thread context from message mappings, and formats outbound messages with author attribution. Returns array of OutboundActions for the BridgeManager to dispatch via adapters. 10 tests covering echo prevention, direction filtering, formatting, thread context, and unmapped hives.

title: Implement BridgeManager service
content:
Implement the `BridgeManager` class as a server-level service that manages bridge adapter lifecycles.

**Responsibilities:**
- Load active bridge configs from DB on startup
- Instantiate correct adapter for each platform
- Wire adapter message stream to inbound pipeline
- Create outbound relay (WebSocket subscription) for each bridge
- Handle adapter reconnection with exponential backoff on error
- Update bridge status in DB (`active`, `inactive`, `error` + error_message)
- Expose `startBridge(id)`, `stopBridge(id)`, `stopAll()`, `getBridgeStatus(id)`
- Respect `config.bridge.maxBridges` limit
- Graceful shutdown — disconnect all bridges on server stop

Create as `src/bridge/manager.ts`. This is the main entry point that the server wires into its lifecycle.

Depends on: bridge DAL, inbound pipeline, outbound pipeline, bridge types, credential encryption, bridge config.

feedback.content: Implemented BridgeManager in `src/bridge/manager.ts`. Features: adapter registry pattern, startBridge/stopBridge/stopAll lifecycle, credential decryption, message loop with AbortController, exponential backoff reconnection, outbound event relay via `notifyHiveEvent()`, mapping hot-reload, startAll for server startup, max bridges limit enforcement. 18 tests covering start/stop lifecycle, credential handling, error cases, limit enforcement, outbound relay, mapping reload, and startAll.

title: Implement bridge API routes
content:
Add API routes for bridge management at `/api/v1/bridges`.

**Endpoints:**
- `POST /api/v1/bridges` — create bridge config (validate platform, transport_mode, credentials)
- `GET /api/v1/bridges` — list bridge configs (redact credentials)
- `GET /api/v1/bridges/:id` — get bridge details (redact credentials)
- `PUT /api/v1/bridges/:id` — update bridge config
- `DELETE /api/v1/bridges/:id` — delete bridge (must be stopped first)
- `POST /api/v1/bridges/:id/start` — start bridge (calls BridgeManager.startBridge)
- `POST /api/v1/bridges/:id/stop` — stop bridge (calls BridgeManager.stopBridge)
- `POST /api/v1/bridges/:id/mappings` — add channel mapping
- `GET /api/v1/bridges/:id/mappings` — list channel mappings
- `DELETE /api/v1/bridges/:id/mappings/:mid` — remove channel mapping
- `GET /api/v1/bridges/:id/agents` — list proxy agents for this bridge

**Webhook receiver:**
- `POST /api/v1/hooks/:platform/:bridge_id` — inbound webhook endpoint for webhook-mode adapters

Authentication: require authenticated agent, bridge owner or admin only.

Create as `src/api/routes/bridges.ts`, register in the main route setup.

Depends on: bridge DAL, BridgeManager, credential encryption.

feedback.content: Implemented bridge API routes in `src/api/routes/bridges.ts`. Endpoints: CRUD for bridges (with credential redaction), start/stop lifecycle, channel mapping management, proxy agent listing. Auth required for all endpoints, owner/admin check for mutations. Registered in `src/api/index.ts` with optional BridgeManager dependency.

title: Implement Slack bridge adapter (Socket Mode + Events API)
content:
Implement the Slack adapter as the first concrete `BridgeAdapter` implementation.

**Outbound mode (Socket Mode):**
- Use `@slack/socket-mode` package to connect via outbound WebSocket
- Authenticate with `app_token` (xapp-...) for Socket Mode connection
- Use `bot_token` (xoxb-...) for Web API calls (sending messages, user info)
- Receive message events, normalize to `InboundMessage` format
- Resolve Slack user mentions (`<@U0XYZ789>`) to display names via `users.info` API
- Extract thread_ts for threading support

**Webhook mode (Events API):**
- Receive HTTP POST events at `/api/v1/hooks/slack/:bridge_id`
- Verify Slack signing secret (HMAC-SHA256 with `X-Slack-Signature` header)
- Handle URL verification challenge
- Process `message` events, normalize to `InboundMessage`

**Outbound (send):**
- Use `@slack/web-api` to post messages via `chat.postMessage`
- Convert markdown to Slack mrkdwn format
- Support threading via `thread_ts` parameter
- Include author attribution in message

Create as `src/bridge/adapters/slack.ts`.

Depends on: bridge adapter interface/types.

feedback.content: Implemented Slack adapter in `src/bridge/adapters/slack.ts`. Features: Socket Mode (outbound transport) via @slack/socket-mode, Web API message sending via @slack/web-api, Slack mention resolution (`<@U...>` → display names), thread_ts threading support, markdown→mrkdwn conversion, user info caching, pushInboundMessage for webhook mode, file attachment normalization. Installed @slack/socket-mode and @slack/web-api packages. 13 tests covering connection modes, message push/consume, sending, threading, markdown conversion, and disconnect behavior.

title: Wire BridgeManager into server startup/shutdown lifecycle
content:
Integrate the BridgeManager into the OpenHive server lifecycle in `src/server.ts`.

- Instantiate BridgeManager if `config.bridge.enabled` is true
- On server start: call `bridgeManager.loadAndStartActiveBridges()` to resume active bridges
- On server shutdown: call `bridgeManager.stopAll()` for graceful disconnect
- Follow the same pattern as the MAP sync listener and SwarmCraft plugin integration

Depends on: BridgeManager, bridge config.

feedback.content: Wired BridgeManager into server lifecycle in `src/server.ts`. Creates BridgeManager when `config.bridge.enabled`, passes to registerRoutes, calls `startAll()` during server start, and `stopAll()` during shutdown. Follows existing SwarmManager/SyncService patterns.
